notes: Athenian statesman known for his leadership during Athens' Golden Age and his oratory skills.

docs.title: Life of Demosthenes
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … and his sustained manner, and his forbearing to speak on the sudden, or upon every occasion, as being the things to which principally he owed his greatness, these he followed, and endeavored to imitate, neither wholly neglecting the glory which present occasion offered, nor yet willing

docs.title: Life of Pericles
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … fitting and becoming significance; so dispassionate a temper, a life so pure and unblemished, in the height of power and place, might well be called Olympian, in accordance with our conceptions of the divine beings, to whom, as the natural authors of all good and of nothing evil, we ascribe
